channel.title: GraphSys '25 on GraphSys
channel.description: Recent content in GraphSys '25 on GraphSys

item.title: Call for Papers
item.description:
Aligned with Euro-Par, the premier European conference addressing all facets of parallel and distributed processing, GraphSys'25 is the first Workshop on Serverless, Extreme-Scale, and Sustainable Graph Processing Systems. GraphSys is a cross-disciplinary meeting venue focusing on state-of-the-art and emerging (future) graph processing systems.

The workshop is designed for researchers, practitioners, and students interested in sustainable graph processing. Participants from academia, industry, (government) research labs, and nonprofit organizations are encouraged to attend and contribute to the GraphSys discussions and collaborations.

item.title: Organizers and Committee
item.description:
Organizers

  • Jože Martin Rožanec, University of Twente, Netherlands / Jožef Stefan Institute, Slovenia
  • Tiziano De Matteis, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam, The Netherlands

Program Committee (in progress):

General chairs:

  • Prof.dr.ir. Radu Prodan, University of Innsbruck, Austria
  • Prof.dr.ir. Alexandru Iosup, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam, The Netherlands

PC chairs

  • Dr. Jože Martin Rožanec
  • Dr. Tiziano de Matteis

PC members

  • Alvarado Rodriguez Oliver (New Jersey Institute of Technology/Hewlett Packard, USA)
  • Bartolini Andrea (University of Bologna, Italy)
  • Beamer Scott (University of California Santa Cruz, USA)
  • Cochez Michael (Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam, The Netherlands)
  • Dumitru Roman (SINTEF AS/OsloMet - Oslo Metropolitan University, Norway)
  • Daniele De Sensi (Sapienza University of Rome, Italy)
  • Elvesæter Brian (SINTEF AS, Norway)
  • Grobelnik Marko (Jožef Stefan Institute, Slovenia)
  • Iosup Alexandru (Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam, The Netherlands)
  • Kharlamov Evgeny (Bosch Center for Artificial Intelligence, Germany)
  • Kristian Rietveld (Leiden University, The Netherlands)
  • Langguth Johannes (Simula Research Laboratory, Norway)
  • Laurentiu Vasiliu (Peracton Ltd., Ireland)
  • Peter Haase (metaphacts GmbH, Germany)
  • Prodan Radu (University of Innsbruck, Austria)
  • Schroeder Daniel (SINTEF AS, Norway)
  • Varbanescu Ana-Lucia (University of Twente/University of Amsterdam, The Netherlands)

item.title: Program
item.description:
Preliminary Program

Time Description
09:00 - 09:05 Opening
09:05 - 10:00 Keynote 1: prof. dr. Jürgen Becker: “Embedded Reliable AI Chip & EDA Technologies for High-Performance & Graph-based System Integration”
10:00 - 10:30 Coffee Break
10:30 - 11:45 Paper Presentations
11:45 - 12:30 Invited Talk 1: prof. dr. Setareh Maghsudi: “Linear Combinatorial Semi Bandit with Causally Related Rewards”
12:30 - 13:30 Lunch
13:30 - 14:15 Invited Talk 2
14:15 - 15:30 Paper Presentations
15:30 - 16:00 Coffee Break
16:00 - 17:00 Keynote 2: prof. dr. Maciej Besta: “Graphs in the LLM Era: Enabling Effective and Efficient LLM Ecosystems”
17:00 - 17:30 Panel
17:30 Closing remarks

item.title: Keynotes
item.description:
GraphSys 2025 will feature two keynotes:

  • “Embedded Reliable AI Chip and EDA Technologies for High-Performance and Graph-based System Integration”, by Jürgen Becker from the Karlsruhe Institute of Technology – KIT, Germany
  • “Graphs in the LLM Era: Enabling Effective and Efficient LLM Ecosystems”, by Maciej Besta from the Scalable Parallel Computing Lab (SPCL) at ETH Zurich

Embedded Reliable AI Chip and EDA Technologies for High-Performance and Graph-based System Integration

Abstract: Increasing emerging fields towards reliable embedded accelerator and processor integration, incl. High-Performance Computing (HPC) systems, are “essential and challenging forces” driving actual and future silicon technologies. This results in heterogenous multi-core (MC), various vector accelerator incl. artificial intelligence (AI) and networked cyber-physical systems (CPS) integration. Here, on one side increasing monolythic integration of cooperating computational and physical sensor components is necessary, e. g. in embedded and smart on-demand automatized environments as diverse as applied in communication/consumer, automotive/transportation, avionics, civil infrastructure, energy, healthcare, manufacturing (Industry 4.0), and also space appliances. Moreover, future complex dynamically adaptive and multi-domain electronic system integration tends to be more and more dependent on distributed real-time and embedded HPC availability. This results in the strong demand of newly operating decentralized/centralized intelligent, interconnected, and silicon technology integrated solutions, subject to increased performance needs to facilitate computationally intensive algorithms, power consumption to be minimized, as well as sufficient degrees of reliability and verifiability to employ digital systems in highly networked as well as safety-critical environments, e. g. in future embedded, incl. 6G, automotive etc. system integration, driven by software-defined approaches. In such high-performance data acquisition systems, real-time interpretation of captured signals is crucial due to limited offline storage and processing capabilities. Large scientific applications, such as particle detectors or massive radar testbeds, e. g. in automotive, communication etc. often capture inherently sparse data for which point cloud networks (PCNs) have shown promising performance in online event processing tasks. Thus, the exploitation of possible parallelism and neural network partitioning by leveraging dynamic dataflow architectures to guarantee real-time requirements is crucial, e. g. by automatizing hierarchical hardware/software co-design incl. hardware accelerator optimization.

item.title: Supporting Projects and Initiatives
item.description:
The GraphSys workshop is linked to the Graph-Massivizer project funded by the Horizon Europe research and innovation program of the European Union for the period 2023-2026. The project aims to develop a high-performance, scalable, gender-neutral, secure, and sustainable platform for massive graph processing.

Organizer

Graph-Massivizer

Graph-Massivizer researches and develops a high-performance, scalable, and sustainable platform for information processing and reasoning based on the massive graph representation of extreme data.

item.title: Previous Editions
item.description:
GraphSys ‘24

The Workshop website is available here. The accepted papers were published at the ICPE Proceedings

GraphSys ‘23

The Workshop website is available here. The accepted papers were published at the ICPE Proceedings
